description_long: This object is a robust, monolithic guard stone carved from a single block of stone, featuring a stylized, abstract human face with pronounced brow ridges, deep-set eyes, a broad nose, and a defined mouth. The surface is weathered and uneven, with visible chipping and erosion, suggesting long-term exposure to the elements. The stone has a mottled gray coloration with darker patches, likely from accumulated grime or mineral deposits. The back and sides are roughly hewn, lacking any detailed features, while the front is more carefully shaped to display the facial motif. The overall form is slightly tapered towards the base, providing stability when installed upright. This guard stone would serve as a protective barrier at the edge of a street or entrance, preventing vehicle encroachment and marking boundaries.
description: A weathered stone guard stone with a stylized human face carved on the front, featuring rough, mottled gray surfaces and signs of age.
description_view_0: The front view shows the stylized face with prominent brow, nose, and mouth details, and the most defined features.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the depth of the facial carving and the rough, unadorned side surface.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a mostly flat, featureless stone surface with visible weathering and no facial details.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the profile of the face and the irregular, chipped stone texture.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the corners of urban streets, at the entrance to driveways, or along the edge of sidewalks to protect buildings and pedestrians from vehicle intrusion.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be installed upright, partially embedded in the ground for stability, with the carved face oriented toward the street or pathway.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and smoothing on the edges and facial features, likely caused by repeated vehicle contact and weathering. Discoloration and surface erosion are present, especially on the upper and front surfaces exposed to rain and pollution.
